comment_text:
With respect, a satisfying mystery should be solveable with all the clues given to the audience before a reveal is made.

As in Stalker being who they are is a disatisfactory resolution, unless they're not the only Stalker and that Stalker was visible since season 1.

Basically, doesn't matter what rollercoaster ride you build, the end destination theoretically should be forseeable with all the information within the narrative. Otherwise it feels disappointing, like the last 2 seasons of A Popular Show where answers to mysteries were pulled out of the ether.

So I hope the mysteries in Wednesday actually land well.

video_title: Wednesday: Season 2 | Part 2 Sneak Peek | Netflix
comment_text: I don’t know why it’s getting so much flash for being split. They’re not that far apart. What’s much more annoying is Netflix rarely ever finishing a series, and also years between releases. Stranger Things will have made 5 seasons with a handful of episodes, compared to Supernatural, which made 10 or 11 seasons with 20ish episodes per year in the same time. Also, what’s the story? 4 episodes in, and there’s not much story yet. If Wednesday isn’t going to be in a “love triangle”…fine, but 4 episodes in, she doesn’t have any foil to make her interesting. Deadpan only works when there’s a foil, and this season hasn’t made one yet. Great characters, great actors, and great director…but so far, not very emotionally investing. I can go back to the old days of waiting on a new episode (or episodes) for a little bit…but not years, and not when Wednesday spends 4 episodes actually being not likable.💁‍♂️
